examples.title: Iterative Randomized Select
examples.description: Non-recursive implementation to avoid stack overflow for large arrays
examples.code:
import random

def randomized_select_iterative(arr, k):
    """Iterative Randomized Select - finds k-th smallest (0-based: k-1)"""
    left = 0
    right = len(arr) - 1
    target = k - 1  # Convert to 0-based
    
    while left <= right:
        # Base case
        if left == right:
            return arr[left]
        
        # Random pivot
        pivot_index = random.randint(left, right)
        pivot_index = partition_iterative(arr, left, right, pivot_index)
        
        # Check if we found the element
        if target == pivot_index:
            return arr[target]
        elif target < pivot_index:
            right = pivot_index - 1
        else:
            left = pivot_index + 1
    
    return -1  # Should not reach here

def partition_iterative(arr, left, right, pivot_index):
    """In-place partition around pivot"""
    pivot = arr[pivot_index]
    arr[pivot_index], arr[right] = arr[right], arr[pivot_index]
    store = left
    
    for i in range(left, right):
        if arr[i] < pivot:
            arr[store], arr[i] = arr[i], arr[store]
            store += 1
    
    arr[store], arr[right] = arr[right], arr[store]
    return store

# Example:
data = [7, 2, 9, 3, 5, 1, 8]
print(f"3rd smallest: {randomized_select_iterative(data, 3)}")  # 3
print(f"Median: {randomized_select_iterative(data, 4)}")  # 5
examples.explanation: This iterative version uses a while loop instead of recursion, converting tail-recursive calls into loop iterations. This prevents stack overflow for very large arrays and has the same O(n) expected time complexity.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - constant extra space (in-place partitioning and loop)
